utility_evaluation.REASONING: There are 5 items in the required format: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. All 5 appear in the final coverage plan. Now check if each is fully specified: 1) health_coverage requires physical and mental health insurance with costs. The plan lists both physical and mental health insurance names and a cost (€165/month). Fully specified. 2) property_coverage requires home/contents insurance with costs. The plan provides home contents insurance description and cost (€60/month). Fully specified. 3) travel_coverage requires travel insurance with costs. The plan provides a specific policy and cost (€50/month). Fully specified. 4) additional_services requires any enhancement services with costs. The plan lists a premium membership with description and cost (€20/month). Fully specified. 5) total_budget requires complete monthly/annual cost breakdown. The plan provides monthly cost before discount, discount applied, and total monthly cost; it lacks annual figures, but the format says monthly/annual, implying either or both could be acceptable as a complete breakdown. The monthly breakdown is complete; thus considered fully specified.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan includes: LifeFirst Standard Plan for both physical and mental health; LifeFirst Standard Coverage for home contents; InsurePlus Standard Plan for travel (annual multi-trip); and Premium Insurance Membership at €20/month. I matched each named item to the ground-truth catalog: LifeFirst Standard Plan (health physical rating 9 and mental rating 9), LifeFirst Standard Coverage (home content rating 9), InsurePlus Standard Plan under mobility_coverage.travel_insurance (rating 10), and Premium Insurance Membership under general_services (rating 6).
